title: Fix OrderStatus enum mismatch: 'awaiting_payment' not defined
description:
CRITICAL production bug: PostgreSQL database contains orders with status='awaiting_payment', but Python enum OrderStatus does not define this value. SQLAlchemy raises LookupError when querying orders.

Stacktrace:
- /app/app/api/v1/admin/router.py:468 in list_orders
- /app/app/api/v1/orders/repository.py:80 in list_all
- Error: sqlalchemy.exc.StatementError: (builtins.LookupError) 'awaiting_payment' is not among the defined enum values. Enum name: orderstatus. Possible values: pending, pending_payment, paid, processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled, refunded

Current enum definition in backend/app/db/models/order.py:
- PENDING = 'pending'
- PENDING_PAYMENT = 'pending_payment'
- PAID = 'paid'
- PROCESSING = 'processing'
- SHIPPED = 'shipped'
- DELIVERED = 'delivered'
- CANCELLED = 'cancelled'
- REFUNDED = 'refunded'

Required actions:
1. Investigate: check if 'awaiting_payment' is a legacy value from OpenCart migration or old code
2. Decide strategy:
   a) Add AWAITING_PAYMENT to OrderStatus enum + Alembic migration to ALTER TYPE orderstatus
   b) OR create Alembic data migration to UPDATE orders SET status='pending_payment' WHERE status='awaiting_payment'
3. Verify no other code references 'awaiting_payment' string literal
4. Ensure Python enum and PostgreSQL enum are synchronized
5. Test admin orders list endpoint works after fix

Files to check:
- backend/app/db/models/order.py (enum definition)
- backend/app/api/v1/orders/repository.py (query logic)
- backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py (list_orders endpoint)
- backend/migrations/versions/* (check for legacy migrations)

This blocks admin panel from showing orders list.
acceptance_criteria: OrderStatus enum in backend/app/db/models/order.py includes all values present in PostgreSQL database
Alembic migration created to synchronize PostgreSQL enum with Python enum
No 'awaiting_payment' string literals in codebase (or all mapped to correct enum value)
pytest tests/ -x -v → all green (if tests exist for orders)
